results.possibleIdentity: Allan L. Griff is a chemical engineer and independent plastics consultant known for his expertise in extrusion technology. Born in New York, he graduated from Cornell University in 1954 with a B.S. in Chemical Engineering. His early career included technical service and development roles at Union Carbide Plastics, where he specialized in extrusion and thermoplastics processes. In 1961, he established himself as a private consultant, founding Edison Technical Services, Inc. based in the New York area. Griff is the author of several foundational texts, including "Plastics Extrusion Technology" (first published in 1962) and "The Plastics Extrusion Operating Manual." His professional work spans material selection, product design, and processing techniques for industries such as packaging, agriculture, and construction. Notably, Griff maintains an interdisciplinary interest in anthropology and the social impact of technology, having contributed to international grain recovery guidelines and written extensively on the "cultural evolution" of science. During the late 1960s and 1970s, he was active in New York as a consultant, an era and location consistent with the activities of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization. In recent years, he has continued his work as an educator and technical advisor while also pursuing personal interests in poetry and proverbs.
